mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising housing starts and expanding real consumer credit indicates accelerating aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving equity multiples higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive forward returns in at least 55% of observations, or the cross-sectional correlation between HOUST/RSAFS and SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to potential output forces firms to raise prices to maintain margins, which sustains inflation expectations and supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the supply contraction signal, or the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO changes and equity returns exceeds 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically lifts broad equity indices over the following 5 trading days as analysts update consensus forecasts.
failure_condition: The hypothesis predicts positive returns but walk-forward analysis shows a negative or statistically insignificant mean forward return across all GDP growth deciles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold while inflation remains sticky, real rates turn deeply positive and compress duration-sensitive equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint high-rate/high-inflation regime fails to produce negative forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or the realized forward return distribution shows no significant left skew relative to the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth combined with stable unemployment indicates a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive equity returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint distribution of PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no predictive power for subsequent equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals an approaching economic contraction that forces downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.0% annualized
failure_condition: The hypothesis predicts negative returns but walk-forward analysis shows a positive or statistically insignificant mean forward return across all GDP growth deciles.
